title: Serverless Computing
author: Priya
content:
Take Quiz

Azure Function/AWS Lambda/Google Cloud Function

  • These r serverless computing modules of cloud which triggers itself by invoking the code written as function to respond to an event.They r called FaaS model(Function as a Service) as it allows developers to write code and they don't need to manage servers by themselves.CSP takes care of the infrastructure management,scaling or any hardware maintenance.Every resource provosioning and maintaining is done by CSP or a 3rd party vendor. Developers only have to focus on writing code and deploying it.That's why they r called serverless even though servers r there for overall management.
  • The serverless functions provide built-in support for different programming languages.We write code on the text editor they provide, whose interface is just like our normal code editor and provide all the library support that we get with a JDK or SDK development toolkit.We can also import the code in zip format from local.And everytime we make any code change , we have to deploy it to save all the newly made changes.
  • If suppose we developed an application using non-serverless architecture , it means we will 1st buy a cloud base server and than will deploy our VM on it than we will set configuration for GBs of RAM & harddisk and high end processing unit as per the requirement of our app.And we will pay for it.If our requirement exceeds than we would buy more amount of computing power(RAM ,storage etc) and will pay for it.So, for these resources , there will be fixed charge allocation on us that we will pay monthly/quarterly or on yearly basis.No matter whether our app is in use or not but we still have to pay for the allocated number of resources.
  • So , to save up money, u can switch to serverless architecture. and don't have to pay any cost where there is no user call and when your app is in idle state with no user accessing it.And in serverless , we will be charged per hour basis only for the duration our code run.If suppose any user access our serverless architecture app than for how long our code run.eg...5sec and than when another user used our app ,it run for 6sec to call certain function.than we will be charged for this 11sec whose amount will be too small.And if our function invocation is done 1 million times, till this limit we don't have to pay any amount but will be charged for 1M+ user call request per month basis.So, it is very cost-effective as we will be charged only when our code/function is called.

    • How GCF is different from other serverless

    1. Google cloud function always keep its function active by running all the resources in the background.So, google CF don't have a cold start.and there is no concurrency limit set for GCF.
    2. There is no throttling issue when burst of user request comes at once.But we can set a request quota by precisely specifying number of request that our function can accept at a time.
    3. It provides less detailed logging and monitoring data as compared to AWS Lambda with no VPC support.

Monolithic Vs Serverless Architecture

By choosing a serverless architecture, the engineers can launch the code easily without the need to handle the servers. It is a FaaS platform and widely use in AWS Lambda/Azure Functions or other event driven platform to create serverless apps. monolithic app happens to be an all-in-one application. It is equipped with different features within a singular codebase, and singular programming language. It can be an ideal choice for simple apps.
Both of these architecture fails if u have a complex application equipped with an ample amount of data related to every user record .It can pose lot of performance problems when u have large userbase with a heavy weight code build as a single unit with chunks of class files grouped together or as a function that has to handle tough events .These architectures r known for their simplicity and for handling simple task that don't require lots of backend processing.

Comparison -> Monolithic provides easier testing and debugging than the serverless architecture.
Cost :- when it comes to the financial considerations of monolith and serverless computing, monolith incur additional costs for scaling up the resources as the demand increase and also u still have to pay incase of when your computing resources is in their idle state and not in use during non-peak periods but with serverless u only have to pay when any events occur .You will only be charged when function written for that events will trigger itself .
Scaling :- Serverless architecture can automatically scale itself during traffic hikes and peak periods. as the number of request increase their functions can serve those request parallely through automatic horizontal scaling managed by the serverless platform.When the function is serving 1 request and at the same time If a new request arrives while an existing instance is busy, the serverless platform automatically provisions a new, independent instance of that function to handle the new request. This process is known as horizontal scaling.
Response time :- Monolith gives faster response times and reduced latency as the components are integrated tightly.In serverless architecture Response times are extremely fast for the active functions. However, it involves latency, or a cold start, which appears after the inactivity. When the code or function written with serverless architecture hasn't taken in use for a longer period than they come at the stage of cold start and may not respond when any events occurred or may not trigger instantly but with a delay to respond to an event.

Cold start

Cold starts in serverless computing refer to the delay that occurs when a serverless function is invoked after being idle or scaled down.To save up cost , cloud service provider shut down resources set for that serverless function and when new request or event occurs than CSP resume those functions by allocating resources, downloading and redeploying that code or function, and start the runtime environment, which adds latency to the request.The concept of this cold start is very much similar to an engine . When u attempt to start a vehicle's engine due to its inactiveness for a longer time which even makes u to put some extra time and effort to make engine run after long time of not using it.

Resource management :- In the case of monolithic when it comes to managing your app there is a need for managing of its hardware or other runtime resources which requires time. So,1 has to keep up with security and software updates, server hardware maintenance, and backup creation everything to give uninterrupted, and easy to use service for the end user. So,there is still a lot of headwin when it comes to giving a reliable experience to the user but in Serverless u only have to worry about your code ,can focus on app creation by offloading such duties of computing/storage or any other hardware components maintenance from your end .You can execute and create the services and apps through the serverless architecture without the need to manage the infrastructure. Though app requires server to operate.

Operations serverless architecture can't handle

Stateful operation - They r designed to trigger instantly when an event occurs but don't retain any of such event data or response after it execute that event .U can see the logs or graphically on cloudwatch for the event and response after the event is completed.But it is not good in dealing with long-term memory operation and may require extra resources to store such events and response which can increase function's complexity and cost.eg...If we create a function to trigger when a user signin to our app it can do so but may not be able to store any detailed user data bcoz using any db for that can make that function heavy to bootstrap itself instantly incase handling any request as making connections to db may require it to establish connection with that db and closing that connection after the request is served .So,boot up time may increased for the function code to trigger itself instantly on an event. These Serverless functions are stateless by default, meaning they don't retain information between invocations. Since,they r designed to handle request with an easy to trigger function.Adding extra libraries or code to handle db related task can make these function more complex and also slow to response.
Spiky, high-frequency workloads - Due to its limitations of cold start making activeness to respond to frequent events may not be possible at all time. While serverless can scale, it may fail to handle a sudden, massive burst of events effectively due to the time it takes to handle new request (due to cold start) and due to set execution limit.The maximum number of events a serverless function like AWS Lambda can handle at any given moment is determined by its concurrency limits, which are 1,000 concurrent executions.So,a serverless function can handle a maximum of 1000 events at a time and additional requests will be rejected or "throttled" if this limit is reached.
Long running task - Serverless functions are designed for short, stateless operations. Long-running processes can lead to timeouts .Most serverless platforms enforce strict maximum execution times for individual functions. For instance, AWS Lambda functions time out after 15 minutes,.
So,they cannot handle task like debugging and troubleshooting. audio/Video encoding ,batch data processing when large number of request comes at once they might become slow to handle them, or complex machine learning workflows which requires work like pattern analysis, auditing of long events .
